eval_protocol: Activate the skill, run the prompt verbatim, grade each scoring_criteria item pass/fail. Eval passes if all criteria met.

evals.prompt: Maak een grafiek van het aandeel eigenaar-bewoners, corporatiehuurders en particuliere huurders per regio.
evals.expected_output: Generates a stacked or grouped bar chart, explicitly avoids pie charts
scoring_criteria: Does NOT generate a pie chart (no alt.mark_arc() without a bar alternative, no plt.pie())
Generates a stacked bar chart OR grouped bar chart OR 100% stacked bar
Explicitly mentions that pie charts are avoided ('geen taartdiagrammen')
Explains why (hard to compare segments, especially with 3+ categories)

evals.prompt: Maak een staafdiagram van het percentage zonnepanelen per provincie.
evals.expected_output: Chart has a journalistic finding as title, subtitle with time period, CBS source line
scoring_criteria: Chart title is a journalistic finding (e.g. 'Zuid-Holland loopt achter op zonnepanelen') NOT just a variable description ('Zonnepanelen per provincie')
Subtitle includes the time period and data source
Source line reads 'Bron: CBS StatLine'

evals.prompt: Maak een horizontaal staafdiagram van gemeenten op zonnepanelen en markeer Utrecht als uitschieter.
evals.expected_output: Altair chart with alt.condition() coloring Utrecht differently, muted grey for others
scoring_criteria: Uses Altair (alt.Chart), not matplotlib or plotly as first choice
Uses alt.condition() or equivalent to color Utrecht differently from other bars
Non-highlighted bars use a muted/grey color (#bdbdbd or similar)
Chart is horizontal (horizontal bars for many categories)
Utrecht's bar uses the focus/highlight color (red or similar)

evals.prompt: Sla mijn Altair-grafiek op voor publicatie in zowel print als web.
evals.expected_output: Saves PNG at high resolution and SVG, creates output directory
scoring_criteria: Saves PNG at high resolution (scale_factor=2 or equivalent)
Saves SVG for vector/print quality
Creates output directory with os.makedirs(..., exist_ok=True)
Both save calls are in the code (not just one format)
Mentions that vl-convert-python is needed for PNG export from Altair

evals.prompt: Genereer alt-tekst voor mijn staafdiagram van zonnepanelen per gemeente.
evals.expected_output: Single Dutch descriptive sentence following the specified format template
scoring_criteria: Produces a single descriptive sentence (not a list of all values)
Alt text mentions the chart type ('horizontaal staafdiagram')
Alt text mentions the value range or key finding
Alt text is in Dutch (since the chart is in Dutch)
Format follows: '[Charttype] van [N] [geografisch niveau] in [land/regio], [tijdperiode]. [Bevinding].'

evals.prompt: Maak een lijndiagram met 5 provincies op één grafiek. Zorg voor toegankelijke kleuren.
evals.expected_output: Uses a named colorblind-safe palette, not red-green, mentions accessibility
scoring_criteria: Uses Tableau10 (Altair default), a colorblind-safe palette, OR names a specific safe palette
Does NOT use a red-green combination as the primary distinction
Assigns colors via a named scale (alt.Color with scale=alt.Scale(scheme=...)) rather than hardcoded hex list
Mentions colorblindness accessibility ('kleurenblindveilig')

evals.prompt: Maak een scatterplot van inkomen vs. zonnepanelen per gemeente.
evals.expected_output: Altair scatterplot as first suggestion, with trend line, no matplotlib default
scoring_criteria: Uses Altair as the primary library (not matplotlib or plotly)
Only suggests plotly if interactivity is mentioned by the user
Does NOT default to matplotlib.pyplot as first suggestion
Adds regression line or trend line if possible in Altair

evals.prompt: Gebruik de journalism-stijlmodule voor mijn Altair-grafiek.
evals.expected_output: Imports journalism_style.py, registers and enables the theme, uses COLORS constants
scoring_criteria: Imports from journalism_style.py (or assets/journalism_style.py)
Calls alt.themes.register('journalism', altair_journalism_theme)
Calls alt.themes.enable('journalism')
Uses COLORS['focus'] for the highlighted data point
Uses COLORS['muted'] (#bdbdbd) for background data points
